scenarios.query:
Audit this compliance scan. Product: closed-source desktop app sold worldwide.
```text
licenses: dep `gpl-tablesort` GPL-3.0, statically linked into the shipped binary
vendored: src/vendor/fastjson/ — files match a GitHub repo, copyright headers removed
pii: crash reporter uploads full memory dumps (may contain user documents) with
     no user prompt; retention unlimited
```
expected_behavior: Flags the GPL-3.0 statically linked into a closed-source shipped binary as a serious license conflict to escalate to legal (not to silently decide)
Flags the vendored code with removed copyright headers as an attribution violation to restore
Flags the crash-dump PII flow (no consent, unbounded retention, sensitive contents) requiring consent gating, minimization, and retention limits

scenarios.query:
Audit this scan. Product: proprietary SaaS.
```text
new code: utils/geo.py — 240 lines matching a Stack Overflow answer verbatim,
  no attribution, no license note
telemetry: new feature logs user GPS coordinates to the analytics pipeline;
  purpose: "may be useful later"; no consent flow; users in EU
sbom: last generated 14 months ago; 60+ dep changes since
```
expected_behavior: Flags the unattributed Stack Overflow code as a provenance/license gap (CC BY-SA obligations) to attribute or rewrite
Flags the GPS collection with no stated purpose, no consent, and EU users as a data-protection violation to escalate (purpose limitation/consent)
Flags the stale SBOM (14 months, 60+ changes) for regeneration

scenarios.query:
Audit this compliance scan. Product: Apache-2.0 open-source CLI.
```text
licenses: deps all Apache-2.0/MIT/BSD; NOTICE file current
spdx: REUSE lint green (100% headers)
pii: tool collects nothing; --telemetry flag exists, defaults off, documented
provenance: no vendored code; SBOM attached to each release
```
expected_behavior: Reports no findings (compliance and provenance are clean)
Recognizes compatible licenses, full attribution/SPDX coverage, opt-in telemetry, current SBOM
Does not speculate about legal risks the scan provides no evidence for
